notes:
Plaintiff opening work notes for arb-ex02-20260629190403.

Opportunity: openings:plaintiff. Final filing action allowed: record_opening_statement only. Evidence admission not allowed in this phase. Attempts remaining 3; text limit 5000; target first submission <=3750 chars.

Record/evidence scan performed: get_case and list_evidence show three juror-visible case-packet files, no submitted evidence, no arguments/openings/technical reports/exhibits. stat_evidence and read_evidence_range used for all three files.

Evidence contents and weight:
- ev_74d969c640f7_market-question, sha256 74d969c640f7b64e403a992ce7247806f20410a537953a56f8d3082642619190, 350 bytes: market title is "What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw?"; strike Ronaldo; challenged resolution No; requested Yes; core dispute is whether "during the FIFA World Cup Draw" includes Trump's public remarks at the venue immediately before the formal draw program began, or only the formal on-stage program.
- ev_7de1d3f2c404_formal-draw-program, sha256 7de1d3f2c4043f0ff9954b10f9e0b3b927cbf654f9bb46a401b40fee3dc46b03, 279 bytes: draft complaint says formal draw-program transcript does not appear to contain "Ronaldo". It is a starting point on the formal-program side and expressly does not claim to be the full formal-program transcript.
- ev_af2671376951_pre-event-remarks, sha256 af267137695148692b861c06fb66ff8168816e28a68b9576eb48eb8388a1bfdc, 344 bytes: draft complaint attributes two quoted lines to Trump's public remarks at the venue immediately before the formal program: "I got to meet Ronaldo the other day." and "Ronaldo is great." It is a starting point and expressly does not claim to be the full upstream transcript.

Source map: decisive elements are (1) market-language boundary for "during the FIFA World Cup Draw"; (2) event/timing connection between venue pre-event remarks and the draw; (3) whether Trump said the strike in the relevant remarks; (4) whether any market rule narrows the event to formal on-stage program. Strongest adverse theory is that only the formal program transcript counts, and that transcript lacks "Ronaldo."

Native investigation before opening: web searched exact market title plus Ronaldo; Trump/FIFA World Cup Draw/Ronaldo is great; exact phrase "I got to meet Ronaldo the other day" with Trump and World Cup Draw. Found Agent Court example index and GitHub source packet for arb/examples/ex02. Upstream README states the packet is normalized from a draft complaint, does not contain downloaded source archives, and did not fetch American Presidency Project, Roll Call/Factba.se, Kalshi page, or Bernard Bulletin while building the example. This confirms provenance gaps and later source targets but is not AAR-admitted evidence. Search also surfaced social/video leads and a Dailymotion transcript-like page; not relying on them as record proof in opening because outside material cannot be admitted now.

Theory: Opening should frame the case as a boundary/interpretation dispute, not a dispute over whether the formal-stage transcript alone says Ronaldo. Plaintiff can fairly argue that if public remarks at the same venue immediately before the formal program were part of the draw event, then the attributed Ronaldo statements satisfy the strike; the current packet already identifies that question and the contrary formal-program point. Opening should acknowledge missing primary sources and preview later evidence targets: market rules/page, full transcript or video/audio of pre-event remarks, timing/program context, and adverse formal transcript/rule checks.

Turn: openings:defendant. Objective: defendant opening statement. Record inspected via get_case/list_evidence. Evidence stat/read completed for all three packet files. Current record has only case-packet files: market-question, formal-draw-program, pre-event-remarks; no submitted evidence/exhibits. Packet contents: market-question frames dispute over whether “during the FIFA World Cup Draw” includes public venue remarks immediately before formal draw or only formal on-stage program. formal-draw-program says draft complaint states formal draw-program transcript does not appear to contain “Ronaldo” and is not full formal transcript. pre-event-remarks attributes two Ronaldo lines to pre-event public remarks but says it is not full upstream transcript. External investigation: searched exact market/title/quotes and primary-source terms. Found Agent Court examples page/GitHub ex02 README indicating the example folder is normalized from a draft and does not contain downloaded source archives; README says APP transcript, Roll Call/Factba.se transcript, Kalshi page, and Bernard Bulletin were not fetched. Found search-result leads to Bernard Bulletin, PBS/YouTube, APP/Factba.se terms, but no material admitted evidence may be cited in opening. Source map for later evidence: Kalshi rules/market page, formal draw transcript, full pre-event recording/transcript, timing proof, adverse rule limiting scope. Defense theory: plaintiff bears burden to show both that Trump said the strike in a qualifying source and that pre-program press remarks count under market rules/timeframe; current record is too thin and actually includes the contrary formal-transcript point. Provenance/custody weakness is central. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff merits work notes for arb-ex02-20260629190403.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Final filing action: submit_argument. Evidence submission allowed; max text 6000; max exhibits 9; max submitted evidence for side 8, 0 used before this turn.

Record scan before filing: get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence performed. Initial record had original three packet files plus plaintiff and defendant openings, no submitted evidence. Defendant opening emphasized formal transcript absence, packet provenance gaps, and plaintiff's burden to show market scope. Packet evidence remains: market-question frames boundary dispute; formal-draw-program says formal transcript does not appear to contain Ronaldo and is not full transcript; pre-event-remarks attributes two Ronaldo lines to pre-event venue remarks and is not full upstream transcript.

Search/source map: decisive issues are (1) whether Trump said Ronaldo; (2) whether remarks were tied to the FIFA World Cup Drawing event; (3) whether they occurred before the formal program; (4) whether market scope excludes pre-program/red-carpet remarks. Strongest adverse source would be official Kalshi rules narrowing to formal program only or an official formal transcript omitting the strike. Search terms included exact market title plus Ronaldo; exact phrases "Ronaldo is great" and "Got to meet Ronaldo"; source targets from upstream packet README: American Presidency Project, Roll Call/Factba.se, Kalshi page, Bernard Bulletin; broader web sources for AP/event context.

Sources found and submitted:
- Senate Democratic Caucus transcript page, "TRANSCRIPT: President Trump Speaks to Reporters at the FIFA World Cup Drawing, 12.5.25". Opened source page. Captured page title, video link note, red-carpet timing note, source-chain note (video/audio pieced from TV Azteca, Telemundo, SBT Media, El Destape; portions inaudible), and two Ronaldo passages. Submitted as ev_6ed0ce37445b_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-6ed0ce37445b. This is strongest source for the actual remarks; limitation is it is a transcript/excerpt capture rather than raw video, and it discloses audio gaps.
- AP News article, "Music, comedy and a whole lot of Trump. And then finally, an actual World Cup draw". Opened source page. Captured event/venue context: Trump at 2026 World Cup draw at Kennedy Center, event/spectacle context, AP photo captions. Submitted as ev_87077c188322_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-87077c188322. Corroborates event setting but not transcript.
- Bernard Bulletin "Kalshi Mention Market Dispute Bible". Opened source page. Captured exact market entry: 12-5-25 Trump/FIFA World Cup Draw, strike Ronaldo, evidence red-carpet prior to programme and livestreamed, rules Version/Timeframe, result N, last trade Y $.10; nearby boundary examples. Submitted as ev_5d340a46bd86_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-5d340a46bd86. Secondary and adverse in result, but useful market-dispute context.

Final list_evidence after submissions confirms the three new submitted evidence IDs are juror-visible. No official Kalshi market page/rules were captured directly in time; Kalshi search result surfaced the market title in tag pages but not full market rules. No official raw video was downloaded. I will not overclaim those missing items. Merits argument should offer PX-1 transcript, PX-2 AP context, PX-3 Bernard ledger, plus original packet files if useful. Argument should concede formal-program absence and provenance limits, but argue preponderance favors Yes because admitted transcript proves Ronaldo was said in public red-carpet remarks tied to the FIFA World Cup Drawing, and no admitted rule narrows "during" to formal on-stage transcript only.

Turn: arguments:defendant. Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence. New plaintiff evidence read byte-for-byte: ev_6ed0ce... Senate Democratic Caucus transcript capture; ev_87077... AP context; ev_5d340... Bernard Bulletin ledger. Analysis: PX-1 likely proves Ronaldo was said in red-carpet reporter remarks, but also says Trump walked red carpet for 18 minutes before the FIFA event, press pool engaged only ~3 minutes, audio/video pieced from TV Azteca/Telemundo/SBT/El Destape/others, portions inaudible, and it is not raw video. PX-2 only proves draw event context and venue, not red-carpet transcript or market rules. PX-3 is secondary but says exact market result was N and rules were Version/Timeframe; it describes the Ronaldo utterance as before the programme. Existing formal-draw packet says formal draw-program transcript does not appear to contain Ronaldo. External checks: searched Kalshi exact title/rules/Version Timeframe. Opened Kalshi tag page; exposed generic page/footer, no official market rules. Tried Kalshi public API markets search with broad and exact title queries; returned unrelated active World Cup sports multigame markets, no clean official mention market/rules capture. No defense evidence submitted because no reliable official rule source captured. Merits strategy: concede utterance likely occurred, attack qualifying scope and burden. Plaintiff has not admitted official Kalshi market page/rules; their own sources call the remarks before/prior to event/programme and report actual result N. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal work notes for arb-ex02-20260629190403.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Final actions allowed: submit_rebuttal or pass_phase_opportunity. Evidence submission allowed, but I do not plan additional evidence because defendant submitted no new source evidence and the rebuttal turns on weight/interpretation of existing admitted exhibits.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence performed. Defendant merits argument offered plaintiff exhibits as DX exhibits and filed a technical search ledger stating it could not capture official Kalshi rule/market material through accessible Kalshi page/API searches. No defendant submitted_evidence items appeared. Evidence metadata for PX-1 Senate transcript and PX-3 Bernard ledger unchanged.

Strongest opposing points: (1) PX-1 states Trump walked red carpet before the FIFA event, so defendant says this is outside the draw; (2) PX-3 states red carpet prior to programme, Version/Timeframe, result N, so defendant says secondary ledger supports No; (3) lack of official Kalshi rule page is plaintiff's burden problem.

Rebuttal analysis: The proposition itself asks whether pre-event remarks at the venue immediately before the formal program count as during the draw. Defendant's argument assumes that only the formal program counts, but no admitted official rule says that. PX-1's 'before FIFA event' note establishes red-carpet timing, but the same source title is 'at the FIFA World Cup Drawing' and AP places Trump at the Kennedy Center draw spectacle. Ordinary event language can include red-carpet/arrival remarks immediately before the formal on-stage program. PX-3 is secondary and adverse on reported result, but it confirms the exact dispute, livestreamed red-carpet evidence, and comparable market-boundary examples; 'Version/Timeframe' is a category label, not rule text. Defendant's failed search is not affirmative evidence of a narrowing rule. Rebuttal should be concise, answer scope/burden, and avoid overclaiming raw video or official rules.

Turn: surrebuttals:defendant. Record checked with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal filed but added no new submitted evidence. Existing evidence unchanged: three plaintiff argument sources plus case packet. Rebuttal’s strongest point: defense concedes utterance and allegedly turns “before formal program” into a narrow exclusion despite the proposition asking event-boundary question; plaintiff says absence of official exclusion plus common-sense event context should suffice. Defense response: burden remains on plaintiff to overturn No and prove affirmative inclusion. Record contains affirmative boundary facts in defense favor: PX-1 says red carpet was before the FIFA event; PX-3 says prior to start of programme and reports Version/Timeframe plus result N; AP only gives venue/event context; formal-program file says no Ronaldo in formal program. No new outside research needed because rebuttal raised no new source/fact; issue is evidentiary/legal weight. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing work notes for arb-ex02-20260629190403.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Final action allowed: deliver_closing_statement. No evidence submission in closing.

Final record scan performed: get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence for PX-1 Senate transcript and formal-program file. Evidence list unchanged: three packet files and three plaintiff submitted-evidence items. Defendant did not add new evidence; defendant surrebuttal reoffered plaintiff evidence and repeated timing/burden argument.

Defendant surrebuttal summary: says plaintiff proved Ronaldo in pre-event red-carpet exchange but not qualifying timeframe; relies on PX-1 'before the FIFA event,' PX-3 'prior to programme' plus Version/Timeframe and result N, and formal-program transcript apparent absence. Argues missing official market rule is plaintiff's risk.

Closing analysis: The decision reduces to three questions: (1) Did Trump say Ronaldo? Yes, PX-1 has two Ronaldo passages and defendant effectively concedes likely utterance. (2) Was the statement connected to the FIFA World Cup Draw event? Yes, PX-1 source title/timing, PX-2 AP Kennedy Center draw context, PX-3 red-carpet/livestream dispute entry. (3) Is there admitted rule text excluding red-carpet/venue remarks immediately before the formal program? No. Defense relies on formal-program absence and secondary adverse result, not actual rule text. Formal-program absence wins only if the council imports a formal-program-only boundary; the proposition asks whether broader pre-event venue remarks count. Best closing: candidly acknowledge PX-1 source limits, PX-3 secondary/adverse, and missing official Kalshi page; argue those limits affect weight but do not defeat preponderance because the central fact is corroborated and no exclusion is proven. Ask council to resolve Yes.

Turn: closings:defendant. Final record checked with get_case/list_evidence after plaintiff closing. Evidence unchanged. Plaintiff closing concedes formal-program packet apparently lacks Ronaldo and argues broader event boundary/no admitted exclusion. Defense closing plan: synthesize burden and admitted facts. Record proves Trump likely said Ronaldo only in red-carpet remarks; PX-1 places red carpet before FIFA event and notes reconstruction/audio gaps; PX-3 says prior to start of programme, Version/Timeframe, result N; formal-program packet says Ronaldo absent from formal program; AP only establishes event context; both parties failed to capture official Kalshi rule text, but that gap hurts plaintiff because proposition seeks reversal to Yes. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
